forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea extends a ridge across South Australia through to Friday. Northerly winds freshen on Friday ahead of a trough that is forecast to move across the far west Friday afternoon, then continue across the state during the weekend.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Mostly sunny. Possible early morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures above average about the agricultural area grading to slightly below average in the far north. Northeast winds tending southeasterly about coastal districts.
Mostly sunny. Possible early fog in the southeast. Maximum temperatures above average. East to northeasterly winds.
Mostly sunny. Maximum temperatures well above average. Northeast to northerly winds, fresh in the west during the afternoon.

text.p: Showers and possible thunderstorms developing west of about Ceduna. Maximum temperatures well above average. Northerly winds freshening in the west ahead of a southerly change in the far west later.
Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: Nothing through to Thursday, then 0-5mm west of Ceduna on Friday.
